name: Education 1St
description_short: Education First Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution established in 1932 serving the Ogden area with savings accounts, certificates, auto loans, and rewards credit cards.
description_long:
Education First Credit Union was established in 1932 and operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution serving the Ogden community. As a credit union, it is NCUA-insured and structured to benefit members rather than external shareholders. The institution positions itself as "#1 Rated Credit Union in Ogden" and emphasizes its mission of empowering the education community.

Education First Credit Union offers a range of deposit products and lending services typical of community credit unions. Their deposit products include Money Market Accounts (as high as 3.85% APY) and 6-month Certificates of Deposit (3.95% APY). On the lending side, they provide new and used auto loans (as low as 4.55% APR for 60-month terms) and Visa Rewards Credit Cards (as low as 11.00% APR). The credit union also provides educational content through articles addressing member questions.

A distinctive feature of Education First Credit Union is their High School Affinity Visa Credit Card program, which donates a portion of each purchase to local high schools. This aligns with their stated mission of supporting the education community and allows members to support their alma maters through routine spending. The credit union also publishes educational articles addressing consumer questions about auto financing and credit union differences from banks.

As a regional credit union, Education First serves a specific geographic and community-focused membership. Members should verify eligibility requirements for membership and compare rates against other institutions. The website provides limited detail on membership criteria, account minimums, or the full range of services available, which is typical for credit union websites focused on member self-service portals.
services: High School Affinity Visa Credit Cards with school donation feature
Educational articles on auto financing and credit union services
pros: Established institution with 92+ years of operating history (founded 1932)
High School Affinity Visa donates portion of purchases to local schools, supporting education community
Money Market Account rates up to 3.85% APY
Auto loan rates as low as 4.55% APR for 60-month terms
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits benefit members
NCUA insurance protection on member deposits
cons: Website lacks details on membership eligibility requirements and how to join
No information on minimum deposits or account opening requirements
Limited transparency on the full range of services available (no mention of PALs, share certificates, or other common credit union products)
Geographic limitation to Ogden area membership unclear from website
No online account opening process or full digital banking details provided
best_for: Ogden-area residents seeking auto financing with competitive rates
Parents, alumni, and students wanting to support local high schools through credit card rewards
Community members who prefer member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions over for-profit banks
diagnosis: Education First Credit Union is best for Ogden-area residents seeking membership in an established, community-focused credit union with competitive auto loan and deposit rates. Primary caveats are the lack of transparency on membership eligibility and the limited service details available on their website—prospective members should contact the institution directly to understand full offerings and eligibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Education 1st — Credit Union in OGDEN, UT | CreditDoc
meta_description: Education 1st — NCUA-insured credit union in OGDEN, UT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
